title: Asplenium asperum
taxonomy: Asplenium asperum is classified within the family Aspleniaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair spleenwort family. This family consists of approximately 700 species of ferns. Asplenium asperum belongs to the genus Asplenium, which is a diverse group of ferns that encompasses over 900 species worldwide. The genus Asplenium is further divided into several subgenera, with Asplenium asperum being a member of the subgenus Asplenium. The classification of Asplenium asperum is consistent with the APG III plant classification system.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating wounds and skin conditions. intro: Asplenium asperum, also known as the Rough Spleenwort, is a species of fern native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce pain and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Asplenium asperum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Asplenium asperum is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments to treat various skin conditions and injuries.
